assertion_results.text: Step 2.3.5 dependency chain context identifies criterion as a dev-only dependency based on its presence in [dev-dependencies] in the manifest
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md 'Dependency Chain Context (Step 2.3.5)' section states: 'Profile: dev-only ([dev-dependencies] in backend/Cargo.toml)' and 'NOT present in production builds -- used for benchmarks only'. Manifest evidence block shows '[dev-dependencies] criterion = "0.5.1"'. Dependency Scope Assessment reaffirms: 'criterion is declared in [dev-dependencies] in backend/Cargo.toml'.

assertion_results.text: The dependency scope decision tree classifies criterion as 'dev-only — not shipped in production' and applies the dev-dependency remediation handling
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 44-50 explicitly reference the decision tree: 'Per the dependency scope decision tree: Dev-only dependencies still represent a supply chain risk... Still create remediation tasks, but with modifications: Add the dev-dependency label, Override priority to Normal regardless of CVE severity, Include a note that the dependency is dev/build-only.' remediation.md line 5-7 confirms: 'criterion is a dev-only dependency (not shipped in production). Per the dependency scope decision tree, remediation tasks are created with the dev-dependency label and Normal priority override.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include the dev-dependency label in the labels array: labels contain 'dev-dependency' alongside 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', and the CVE ID
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 labels: 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-99001, dev-dependency' (line 16) and Jira creation array: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-99001", "dev-dependency"] (line 81). Task 2 labels identical (line 94) and Jira creation array identical (line 150). All four required labels present in both tasks.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task priority is set to Normal regardless of the CVE severity (CVSS 5.3 Medium) — the priority override is applied per the decision tree
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 priority: 'Normal' (line 19), Jira creation priority: 'Normal' (line 83). Task 2 priority: 'Normal' (line 97), Jira creation priority: 'Normal' (line 153). Dev-Dependency Modifications Summary (lines 189-191): 'Priority: Overridden to Normal regardless of CVE severity (CVSS 5.3 Medium). Standard triage would inherit the CVE priority; dev-dependency scope reduces this to Normal because the risk is supply chain only.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description includes a note indicating the dependency is dev/build-only and not shipped in production
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 description (lines 38-39): 'This dependency is dev/build-only and is not shipped in production. Remediation priority is Normal (supply chain risk only).' Task 1 implementation notes (lines 53-56): 'This is a dev-only dependency ([dev-dependencies] in backend/Cargo.toml), used for benchmarks only. It is not shipped in production builds.' Task 2 description (lines 114-115): 'This dependency is dev/build-only and is not shipped in production. Remediation priority is Normal (supply chain risk only).'
